name: Empowerment Community Development
description_short: Not-for-profit federal credit union in Houston offering auto loans, personal loans, and share-secured loans with member ownership benefits.
description_long:
Empowerment Community Development Federal Credit Union (ECDFCU) is a not-for-profit financial cooperative headquartered in Houston, Texas at 6011 West Orem. As a federally-chartered credit union, it operates under the cooperative ownership model where members become shareholders in the institution rather than serving as customers of a for-profit entity.

ECDFCU offers multiple loan products including auto loans for new and used vehicles, personal loans for various purposes, and share-secured loans. They provide financial counseling services to members and emphasize streamlined lending processes. Membership requires a $25 opening deposit and $5 one-time membership fee to a share/savings account, with lifetime membership eligibility extended to immediate family members regardless of employment or relocation changes.

The credit union distinguishes itself through its not-for-profit structure where earnings above required reserves are returned to members as lower loan interest rates and competitive savings dividends. Members maintain ownership control and the institution operates solely for member benefit rather than shareholder profit. The cooperative model creates alignment between the institution's financial performance and member benefits.

ECDFCU is a straightforward community credit union with standard loan offerings and member-centric governance. However, the website provides limited detail on specific interest rates, loan terms, maximum loan amounts, or detailed eligibility requirements. The restricted office hours (Monday-Friday 3pm-5pm) may create accessibility challenges for working members, and the limited online information may require direct contact for comprehensive product details.
services: Auto loans for new and used vehicles
Personal loans for various borrowing needs
In-person office services at Houston location
pros: Not-for-profit structure returns earnings to members as lower rates and competitive dividends
Lifetime membership for members and immediate family, regardless of job changes or relocation
Low membership barrier with $25 opening deposit and $5 one-time fee
Offers financial counseling services to support member financial wellness
Share-secured loans available with competitive rates and continued dividend earnings
Auto loans for both new and used vehicles with competitive rates
Member-owned cooperative structure ensures decisions prioritize member interests
cons: Limited office hours (Monday-Friday 3pm-5pm only) reduce accessibility for working professionals
Website lacks specific APR rates, loan amounts, or detailed terms for any products
No mention of online banking capabilities, mobile apps, or digital-first services
Minimal information about personal loan use cases, maximum amounts, or approval criteria
No transparent fee schedule or comparison tools available on website
best_for: Houston-area residents seeking member-owned lending with lower rates and dividend returns
Individuals with stable local employment who can access limited office hours
Borrowers prioritizing cooperative values and member ownership over traditional banking
diagnosis: ECDFCU is best for Houston-area members seeking a traditional not-for-profit credit union experience with member ownership benefits and lower loan rates. The primary caveat is that the website provides minimal transparency on specific terms, rates, and products—requiring prospective members to call or visit the office for detailed information, which may be inconvenient given the restricted 3pm-5pm hours.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: Empowerment Community Development — NCUA-insured credit union in Houston, TX. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
